q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Parser configured with no_network=False, load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, enabling network I/O for external entity/DTD resolution during XML parsing.

mock_guidance.target: Mock the etree.fromstring call on line 164 to prevent actual XML parsing with the unsafe parser.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the raw bytes passed as the first argument to etree.fromstring (i.e., the result of await request.body()) to verify malicious payloads are correctly rejected or sanitized.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a safe, dummy lxml ElementTree node (e.g., etree.Element('safe_root')) so that the subsequent doc.tag access and return statement succeed without error.
notes: The unsafe parser is created on line 163 and passed to fromstring on line 164; mocking fromstring itself is simplest.
Alternatively, patch etree.XMLParser to force no_network=True, load_dtd=False, resolve_entities=False, and let parsing proceed safely if structural coverage of the rest of the function is needed.
